name: Quimet & Quimet
summary: A classic Poble-sec tapas-and-montaditos stop close to Montjuic, but its weekday-only hours make it more useful before or after race weekend than on the weekend stages.
whyItMatters: It is memorable, fast, and geographically relevant to Montjuic, yet the official hours are a planning constraint that should be visible early.
pros: Official site says the family tavern dates to 1914
Specializes in tapas, montaditos, preserves, vermouth, and wine
No reservation process means it can work as a spontaneous snack when open
cons: Official site says Monday-Friday only, so closed for Saturday Stage 1 and Sunday Stage 2 under normal hours
First-come-first-served and popular, so waits are likely

logistics.reservation_needed: No; official site says no reservations and first-come-first-served service.
logistics.takeaway_options: Unknown; best treated as a standing/casual tapas stop.
logistics.distance_from_base: Unknown until lodging is confirmed; in Poble-sec.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Close to Montjuic/Poble-sec access, exact distance depends on viewing point.
logistics.transit: Poble-sec area; verify race-week station and walking access.
logistics.road_closure_notes: No specific closure information found for this business.

timing.best_visit_window: Friday pre-race lunch or evening, or another weekday Barcelona window.
timing.race_day_risk: High for Stage 1/Stage 2 because normal hours do not include weekends.

sources.title: Quimet & Quimet official website
sources.note: Supports address, hours, no-reservations policy, history since 1914, and tapas/montaditos focus.

open_questions: Verify whether any special Tour de France weekend hours are announced closer to July 2026.
Confirm exact walking route from the selected Montjuic viewing location.
